title: Netavark interface
body:
#### Make networking code reusable 

To prevent code duplication when creating new network backends move
reusable code into a separate internal package.

This allows all network backends to use the same code as long as they
implement the new NetUtil interface.

#### netavark network interface

Implement a new network interface for netavark.
For now only bridge networking is supported.
The interface can create/list/inspect/remove networks. For setup and
teardown netavark will be invoked.


#### netavark IPAM assignment

Add a new boltdb to handle IPAM assignment.

The db structure is the following:
Each network has their own bucket with the network name as bucket key.
Inside the network bucket there is an ID bucket which maps the container ID (key)
to a json array of ip addresses (value).
The network bucket also has a bucket for each subnet, the subnet is used as key.
Inside the subnet bucket an ip is used as key and the container ID as value.

The db should be stored on a tmpfs to ensure we always have a clean
state after a reboot.

comments.body: @baude @mheon @flouthoc @lsm5 You can run the tests with something like `sudo NETAVARK_BINARY=/path/to/netavark   go test -v -mod=vendor -cover ./libpod/network/netavark/`. Note that you have to run this as root because test will create network separate namespaces to prevent leaking interfaces.

I enabled the trace logging level for the tests, however it looks like the netlink rust library prints a ton of debug/trace logs. I think we need to turn this off for netlink somehow because this is really not useful for us or users.

Edit: added the full log [podman-netavark-log.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/7435885/podman-netavark-log.txt)

> I enabled the trace logging level for the tests, however it looks like the netlink rust library prints a ton of debug/trace logs. I think we need to turn this off for netlink somehow because this is really not useful for us or users.
> 
> Edit: added the full log [podman-netavark-log.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/7435885/podman-netavark-log.txt)

OK, I have a fix in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/12131/commits/24b3bf03b9c9535ac64e7c4f38471c5629a3dc4a

@baude @mheon PTAL I think this should be ready. The default network backend is still cni. You can change it via config or `--network-backend` flag to netavark. You can check the used backend with `podman info --format {{.Host.NetworkBackend}}`
The netavark tests that need to execute netavark will be skipped.
